title: Evaluating and Improving Large Language Models for Competitive Program Generation
summary: This study evaluates and improves the ability of Large Language Models to generate code for competitive programming problems. A benchmark of 80 problems was curated from 2024 regional ICPC/CCPC contests, and DeepSeek-R1 achieved fully correct solutions on only 5 out of 80 problems using basic prompts. The authors developed a fine-grained error taxonomy covering both general coding errors and specialized algorithmic errors for the failed cases. An improvement framework was proposed that combines multi-turn dialogue-based repair with information-augmented regeneration, successfully increasing the number of accepted solutions from 5 to 46.
author: Minnan Wei, Ziming Li, Xiang Chen, Menglin Zheng, Ziyan Qu, Cheng Yu, Siyu Chen, Xiaolin Ju
